name: Achievement Unlocked: Unlocking Achievement
definition: The user unlocks their first achievement.

name: Local Legend
definition: The user keeps the app running locally for 30 days without enabling any cloud sync or account-based features.
exclusions: cloud_sync_enabled == true

name: The Vibes Compiled
definition: The user has their first coding-related conversation indexed from a supported AI tool.
signals: coding_terms: code, app, bug, test, build, feature, repo, component, function, API
exclusions: conversation is only about non-coding use

name: Prompt It Into Existence
definition: The user starts a conversation that asks an AI tool to build, create, scaffold, or make something new.
signals: user_intent_terms: build, create, make, scaffold, generate, implement
creation_target_terms: app, tool, site, feature, component, script, plugin
exclusions: phrases appear only in copied documentation or error output

name: Weekend MVP Energy
definition: The user describes a project as an MVP, prototype, quick build, side project, or weekend project.
signals: mvp_terms: MVP, prototype, quick build, side project, weekend project, hackathon, proof of concept, POC
exclusions: terms appear only in unrelated quoted text

name: README-Driven Development
definition: The user spends a conversation defining a project through a README, spec, plan, product brief, or requirements document before implementation begins.
signals: planning_terms_seen: README, spec, plan, product brief, requirements, design doc
implementation_terms_seen_later: build, implement, code, scaffold, create files
exclusions: planning terms appear after implementation terms only

name: The First Big Door
definition: An early project thread carries unusually large context or setup volume, suggesting the user has opened the door on a substantial project or feature.
signals: or thread_character_count >= large_thread_character_threshold when token count is unavailable
exclusions: large pasted logs without project setup or implementation discussion

name: Side Quest Accepted
definition: The user starts a new project or feature while another active project already exists.
exclusions: new project is the same as an existing project alias

name: Main Quest? Never Heard of Her
definition: The user works across 10 distinct project histories within one week.
exclusions: projects without coding-related activity

name: Keeper of Small Fires
definition: The user has five active projects with recent AI coding activity.
exclusions: projects with only app setup or source indexing activity

name: One More Prompt
definition: The user continues the same thread for 10 or more user turns.

name: Actually, Wait
definition: The user changes direction mid-thread with language such as 'actually', 'wait', 'never mind', 'scratch that', or 'instead'.
signals: correction_terms: actually, wait, never mind, scratch that, instead, hold on, no let's, change of plans
exclusions: terms appear in assistant text only
terms appear in quoted examples only

name: The Message Had Mass
definition: A single user or assistant message is unusually long compared with normal conversation messages.
exclusions: generated file contents or large pasted logs can be optionally ignored if needed

name: Confidence High, Context Low
definition: A long thread continues after the conversation appears to be running low on context, memory, or token budget.
signals: context_terms_seen: context window, token limit, running out of context, memory, compact, summarize
exclusions: context terms appear only in unrelated docs

name: Context Window Sunset
definition: The conversation explicitly mentions context limits, compaction, memory loss, token limits, or needing to summarize before continuing.
signals: context_terms: context limit, context window, compaction, compacted, memory loss, token limit, summarize before continuing, out of context
exclusions: terms appear only in a source-code identifier

name: Token Budget Lifestyle
definition: The user discusses tokens, cost, model budget, usage limits, or context management as part of the coding workflow.
signals: budget_terms: token, tokens, cost, usage limit, rate limit, budget, context management, remaining context
exclusions: terms appear only in package names or unrelated copied data

name: The App Has Opinions
definition: The assistant pushes back, recommends a different approach, or warns against the user's proposed direction, and the thread continues productively.
signals: explicit_assistant_pushback_seen: I would avoid, I recommend against, I do not recommend, that is risky, a safer approach, instead I suggest
exclusions: assistant merely lists neutral alternatives without warning or recommendation language

name: Lore Drop
definition: The user provides a large amount of background, project history, or prior decision context before asking for a concrete change.
signals: background_terms: context, history, previously, decision, rationale, background, here's what happened
request_terms_later_or_same_message: can you, please, build, fix, change, implement
exclusions: large pasted logs without narrative context

name: Stack Trace Oracle
definition: The user shares a stack trace, traceback, compiler error, runtime error, or failed command output in a coding thread.
signals: error_patterns: Traceback, stack trace, Exception, Error:, TypeError, ReferenceError, SyntaxError, failed, exit code, panic, fatal
exclusions: assistant-generated hypothetical examples only

name: The Bug Has A Shape Now
definition: A vague issue becomes a concrete error, failing test, reproduction step, or suspected root cause within the same thread.
signals: vague_problem_terms_seen: broken, weird, not working, issue, something's wrong
concrete_debug_terms_seen_later: error, failing test, repro, root cause, because, culprit, line
exclusions: no concrete debug signal appears after vague problem signal
drop_reason: Too semantic for a lightweight detector: requires judging whether a vague issue became concrete.

name: One More Run
definition: The same thread includes repeated fix, run, fail, adjust, and retry language.
signals: iteration_terms: fix, run, retry, again, still failing, adjust, rerun, test again, try this
exclusions: terms appear only in a checklist without actual debugging context

name: Green Bar Acquired
definition: A test, build, lint, or verification failure is followed later by pass/success language in the same thread.
signals: verification_terms: test, build, lint, typecheck, CI, check
exclusions: success phrase appears before the failure only

name: Green By Coincidence
definition: The user or assistant indicates that something passed or started working despite uncertainty about why.
signals: uncertain_success_phrases: not sure why this works, somehow it works, somehow fixed, no idea why, works now somehow, inexplicably passing
exclusions: uncertainty and success terms are unrelated or far apart

name: Understanding Optional
definition: The conversation includes language such as 'I don't know why this works', 'not sure why', or 'somehow this fixed it'.
signals: uncertainty_phrases: I don't know why, not sure why, somehow this fixed it, somehow works, unclear why
exclusions: phrases are used hypothetically rather than about the current work

name: We Are So Back
definition: Failure, brokenness, or error language is followed later by success, fixed, passing, or working language in the same conversation.
signals: failure_terms: broken, failing, error, doesn't work, stuck
success_terms: fixed, works, passing, green, solved
exclusions: success terms refer to a different unrelated topic

name: It's So Over
definition: A thread contains repeated failure, brokenness, or error language without a later success or recovery moment.
exclusions: thread is still active
later recovery is found in same project within 24 hours

name: Rubber Duck With A GPU
definition: The user uses an AI tool to reason through a bug or design problem and reaches a conclusion without asking for a code change.
signals: reasoning_terms_seen: think through, reason about, explain, help me understand, debug conceptually
conclusion_terms_seen_later: makes sense, figured it out, conclusion, decision, root cause
exclusions: thread contains explicit edit, patch, implement, write code, or file-change requests
notes: Keep as a charming imperfect achievement. It should use conservative explicit phrases and may be rare.

name: The Fix Was Elsewhere
definition: The thread starts by investigating one area but later resolves or shifts toward a different file, subsystem, dependency, or assumption.
signals: shift_terms_seen_later: actually in, turns out, root cause was, elsewhere, different file, dependency, assumption
exclusions: only one area or file is mentioned
drop_reason: Too dependent on reliable file, subsystem, or topic comparison.

name: It Works, Therefore It Is
definition: Implementation or fix language is followed by 'it works', 'working', 'fixed', or similar success language.
signals: success_terms_seen_later: it works, working, fixed, solved, done, passing
exclusions: success terms appear before implementation or fix terms only

name: Nobody Touch It
definition: The conversation includes 'it works' plus language suggesting not touching, changing, refactoring, or questioning it further.
signals: success_phrase_seen: it works, working now, fixed
do_not_touch_terms_nearby: don't touch, leave it, don't change, no refactor, stop here, ship it
exclusions: do-not-touch terms refer to unrelated files or instructions

name: Ship It Before It Notices
definition: The user moves from fix/success language to ship, deploy, release, merge, or send-it language in close succession.
signals: ship_terms_seen_later: ship, deploy, release, merge, publish, send it, PR
exclusions: ship terms appear before success terms only

name: LGTM From The Void
definition: The conversation includes review or approval language such as 'LGTM', 'looks good', 'ship it', or 'approved'.
signals: approval_terms: LGTM, looks good, ship it, approved, good to merge, ready
exclusions: approval terms appear only as examples or quoted templates

name: The Diff Looked Friendly
definition: The user accepts or discusses code changes while tests, verification, or review are not mentioned in the same thread.
signals: code_change_terms_seen: diff, patch, changes, edit, modified, implementation
acceptance_terms_seen: looks good, okay, done, great
exclusions: tests or verification are mentioned in adjacent same-project thread within 24 hours
drop_reason: Based on absence of tests or review, which is both noisy and a little too judgmental.

name: Production Is A Place
definition: A lightweight prototype, MVP, or experimental project is discussed together with production, deployment, release, or real users.
signals: prototype_terms: prototype, MVP, quick, side project, experiment, hack
production_terms: production, deploy, release, real users, launch, live
exclusions: production terms refer to a different established project

name: The Button Exists Now
definition: The user asks for, receives, or iterates on a concrete UI control such as a button, modal, sidebar, dropdown, toggle, or settings panel.
signals: ui_control_terms: button, modal, sidebar, dropdown, toggle, checkbox, settings panel, menu, tab
exclusions: terms appear only in non-UI context

name: CSS Negotiations
definition: A frontend thread includes multiple rounds of styling, layout, spacing, alignment, color, responsive, or visual polish adjustments.
signals: frontend_terms_seen: CSS, styling, layout, responsive, component, UI
style_adjustment_terms: spacing, alignment, color, padding, margin, font, overlap, polish
exclusions: style terms appear only in generated CSS without discussion

name: Cache Clearing Ritual
definition: The conversation includes clearing cache, restarting a server, reinstalling dependencies, deleting build artifacts, or similar ritual troubleshooting.
signals: ritual_terms: clear cache, restart server, reinstall, delete node_modules, clean build, remove dist, reset cache, npm install, pnpm install
exclusions: terms appear only in setup docs without a troubleshooting context

name: rm -rf
definition: The conversation includes `rm -rf`, destructive cleanup, wiping generated files, deleting dependencies, starting over, or removing a broken local state, followed later by rebuild, recovery, reinstall, or success language.
signals: destructive_cleanup_seen: rm -rf, delete node_modules, wipe, nuke, remove generated, start over, clean slate
recovery_terms_seen_later: reinstall, rebuild, regenerate, restore, works, fixed, passing
exclusions: destructive command appears only inside a warning about what not to do

name: Multiclassing
definition: The user works with both Claude and Codex on the same calendar day.
exclusions: activity is only source indexing with no user conversation

name: Party Finder
definition: The same project has meaningful activity in two supported AI coding tools.
exclusions: project identity confidence is low

name: Changed Lanes
definition: The user switches between Claude and Codex multiple times in one day.
exclusions: switches are caused only by historical backfill ordering

name: Co-op Campaign
definition: The same project has a continuing history across both Claude and Codex.
exclusions: only one-off duplicate import with no continued activity

name: The Council Has Convened
definition: Once a third source is supported, the same project has activity across three AI tools.
exclusions: third source is not yet supported
project identity confidence is low

name: Two Opinions Enter
definition: The user asks two different AI tools about the same project, feature, bug, or decision.
signals: or explicit_second_opinion_terms_seen: Claude said, Codex said, another model suggested, second opinion, what does another model think
exclusions: topic overlap is only generic terms like app, code, bug
source labels alone do not count as explicit second-opinion language
notes: Loved achievement; keep despite imperfect detection. Prefer conservative unlocks over false positives.

name: Model Diplomat
definition: The user reconciles or compares advice from different tools or models in one project journey.
signals: comparison_terms: Claude said, Codex said, another model, different model, compare, reconcile, disagreement, which approach
exclusions: tool names appear only in source labels or app names without comparison language

name: Same Quest, Different Campfire
definition: The user returns to the same project in a different AI tool after at least one day.
exclusions: same-day tool switching only

name: Shipwright
definition: The user discusses committing, merging, releasing, deploying, publishing, or otherwise shipping work.
signals: ship_terms: commit, PR, pull request, merge, release, deploy, publish, launch, shipped, send it
exclusions: terms appear only in unrelated documentation or examples

name: Again, But Different
definition: After a project reaches shipping or completion language, the user starts a new project or a major new version.
signals: new_project_seen_later_or_major_version_terms_seen: v2, rewrite, next version, new project, start fresh
exclusions: new work is only a small follow-up fix to the same shipped project

name: Found Your Way Back
definition: The user returns to a project after seven or more days without detected activity.
exclusions: previous inactivity caused only by missing source permissions or disabled watcher

name: Platinum Memory
definition: The user unlocks every V1 achievement.
exclusions: future or retired achievements are not counted unless part of active V1 set
